title: Dataset v5 large — 100-run production-scale corpus with 27 families on upgraded telemetry
description: Per dataset-todo.md Phase D4.1 sizing: 100 runs = 8 control + 14 compact-a + 14 compact-b + 11 new-families-a + 11 new-families-b + 8 long-running + 24 orphans + 10 system-faults. Builds on v4-large's 13 families with 8 new D1 families (post-deploy-churn, recovered-in-window, single-pod-restart-healthy-replication, third-party-blip, scheduled-job-spike, latency-near-miss-partial-recovery, flapping-pod, slow-leak-saturation), 1 new D12 family (email-outage), and 5 new D11 system-fault families (dns-outage, network-partition, network-packet-loss, network-latency, resource-saturation) for 27 families total. 24 orphan runs contribute 192 orphan ticket_worthy windows (under the D12 200-window target by 8 — acceptable for v5; v5.1 can top up). 10 system-fault runs contribute 50 system-fault ticket_worthy/borderline windows (under the dataset-todo 'D11 >=14 scenarios / >=200 windows' target — see harness_caveats). Collected against the M0-M5 upgraded telemetry layer (L1 shared interceptors, L2 dep-error logs, L3 business events, RecordError fleet-wide, RED + per-dependency + business + runtime metrics, PVC-backed Loki). Estimated runtime: 4-5 days on the GCP VM.

collection_strategy.why_batch_collection: Batch-of-4 first to confirm the new D1 plans run cleanly end-to-end against the upgraded telemetry. Batch-of-10 thereafter so a single-run failure doesn't waste a half-day. The 8 long-running runs are split across the schedule so any leak-scenario harness bug surfaces early instead of after 5 days.

expected_full_scale.long_window_episodes: approx 96 (long-running's 2 fault episodes per run x 8 runs x 6 windows incl. extended observation)
expected_full_scale.orphan_ticket_worthy_windows: 192 (24 orphan runs x 8 orphan ticket_worthy windows per run; close to but slightly under D12 200-window target — v5.1 can top up)
expected_full_scale.system_fault_active_windows: 50 (10 system-faults runs x 5 chaos active_fault windows per run; under the dataset-todo D11 200-window target — this is a first-batch coverage, deferred to v5.1 for full scale)

plans.purpose: Pure baseline runs (no faults). Anchor false-positive distribution and add noise-class training data.
Latency, dependency outage, restart, Redis degradation, bad configuration, near-miss coverage from the v4 family set.
Service-diverse outages, frontend and Redis restarts, intermittent Redis failure, noisy traffic coverage from the v4 family set.
v5 D1.1-D1.4 families: post-deploy-churn, recovered-in-window, single-pod-restart-healthy-replication, third-party-blip.
v5 D1.5-D1.7 families: scheduled-job-spike, latency-near-miss-partial-recovery, flapping-pod.
v5 D1.8 slow-leak-saturation. ~30 minutes per run; contributes the long-window (>=10 min) episodes required by dataset-todo sizing.

Phase D12: 6 near-twin + 2 far orphan scenarios with produces_jira_ticket:false. 8 orphan ticket_worthy windows per run x 24 runs = 192 orphan windows. Enables the orphan_fault_detection benchmark track and the orphan-detection recall gap metric.

Phase D11: 5 chaos-mesh injected system-level faults per run (DNS, network partition, packet loss, network latency, memory pressure). 5 chaos active_fault windows per run x 10 runs = 50 system-fault windows. PREREQUISITE: chaos-mesh installed in the cluster (chaos-testing namespace + CRDs). See docs/gcp-production-dataset-vm-runbook.md 'Install chaos-mesh'.

benchmark_tracks.description: Primary task: classify each telemetry window as ticket_worthy / borderline / noise.
Combined task: classification grounded in Jira memory corpus retrieval. Requires triage_evidence_text.
Secondary task: given a Jira issue, rank candidate telemetry episodes.
Phase D12 task: detect ticket-worthy windows that have NO paired Jira shadow row (produces_jira_ticket=false scenarios).
Measure inter-rater reliability on the 22+% of windows labelled borderline; v5 contributes more borderline shapes from D1.2, D1.4, D1.6.

promotion_gates.raw_runs: Every collected run must pass validate-dataset-run.ps1 with 0 errors and validate-run-feature-distribution.py with 0 anomalies flagged.
promotion_gates.derived_outputs: Per-run triage_examples.jsonl must populate all FEATURE_COLUMNS with >=1 non-zero value across the run, and label distribution must include ticket_worthy + borderline + noise (control + long-running runs are exempt for their respective unused labels).
promotion_gates.aggregate_outputs: Global triage dataset must include all 21 scenario families across runs; new feature columns from M0-M5 upgrade (rpc_server_duration_seconds_*, host_metrics_*, payments_total, etc.) must populate on >=80% of windows; no feature column may be perfectly correlated with scenario_id, scenario_family, or triage_label (leakage canary).

harness_caveats: Single-pod restart (D1.3) scenarios use RestartPods which recycles all matching pods; for true 1-of-N behaviour the deployment must be at >=2 replicas pre-scenario AND a future chaos-mesh PodChaos with one=true selector should replace RestartPods.
Flapping-pod (D1.7) scenarios currently fire a single restart; a wrapper script `scripts/research-lab/flap-pods.ps1` should be authored to call RestartPods 3-5 times with configurable frequency.
Latency-partial-recovery (D1.6) uses brief ScaleDeployment 0; true latency injection requires chaos-mesh NetworkChaos or per-pod sidecar.
Slow-leak-saturation (D1.8) uses long ScaleDeployment 0 to approximate the eventual outage; the saturation curve won't be realistic until a memory/connection-leak injection mechanism exists.
Scheduled-job-spike (D1.5) is a loadgenerator restart approximation; a proper CronJob + resource ballast would be more realistic.

Phase D11 system-faults plan REQUIRES chaos-mesh installed in the cluster (chaos-testing namespace + NetworkChaos / StressChaos CRDs). Without chaos-mesh those 10 runs will fail at apply-manifest time. Install via the runbook 'Install chaos-mesh' section.

Phase D11 covers only 5 of the 7+ scenarios from dataset-todo D11 catalog. IOChaos (D11.7 disk latency) needs sidecar injection setup; TimeChaos (clock skew) needs privileged init; disk-fill (D11.8) needs a PV that v5-large doesn't provision. Deferred to v5.1.
These caveats are noted in each scenario YAML's description field.
